data.title: Many hemp-derived THC products face U.S. ban as Trump signs spending bill
data.description:
Shares of Canadian cannabis companies traded lower in the premarket on Thursday after U.S. President Donald Trump signed a spending bill to end the weeklong government shutdown with provisions to recriminalize hemp THC products from next year.

Trump signed the funding package late Wednesday after the House passed it by 222 votes to 209 earlier in the day, paving the way for the reopening of the federal government following a 43-day shutdown, the longest in U.S. history.

However, the Senate cleared the package on Monday with a last-minute provision to recriminalize many hemp-derived products from late 2026, reversing their legalization under the 2018 Farm Bill.

The spending package “prevents the unregulated sale of intoxicating hemp-based or hemp-derived products, including Delta-8, from being sold online, in gas stations, and corner stores, while preserving non-intoxicating CBD and industrial hemp products,” according to a document provided by the Senate Appropriations Committee.

Signed during Trump’s first term in office, the 2018 Farm Bill legalized products containing less than 0.3% delta-9 THC, the main psychoactive compound in the cannabis plant.  

Following its enactment, “the cannabis industry began producing certain cannabis products that contain less than 0.3% delta-9 THC but a total THC concentration that exceeds 0.3%,” according to a 2025 Congressional summary of the bill. “Some of these products may be intoxicating to the user.”

Non-cannabis players also entered the market, with Retail giant Target (TGT) launching a test run for hemp-derived THC beverages at several Minnesota stores this year. This week, Canadian cannabis company Tilray (TLRY) criticized the hemp-related provision included in the funding bill.

“The hemp language buried within the government funding bill is misguided, out of touch with consumer interests, and misplaced in legislation where it does not belong,” Sam Garfinkel, Tilray’s (TLRY) Senior Vice President, said in a statement on Tuesday.

Other cannabis players offering hemp-derived THC products include Canopy Growth (CGC), Curaleaf (OTCPK:CURLF), Organigram (OGI) and Trulieve Cannabis (OTCQX:TCNNF).

Dear readers, We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: First Mining Gold GAAP EPS of -C$0.02
data.description: • First Mining Gold press release (FFMGF): Q3 GAAP EPS of -C$0.02.

data.title: Intchains Group to acquire proof-of-stake technology platform, expand blockchain infrastructure capabilities
data.description:
• Intchains Group (ICG) on Thursday announced the signing of a definitive agreement to acquire a Proof-of-Stake technology platform for $1.3 million, from ECHOLINK.
  • The acquisition is expected to close in approximately 30 business days after the relevant closing conditions are met and does not include the DxPool brand, or any of Seller’s debts and liabilities, which will remain with the Seller.
  • Upon completion of the acquisition, Intchains intends to leverage both the newly acquired PoS platform and the FalconX platform to stake the bulk of its ETH holdings.
  • ICG +11.11% premarket to $1.2.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cost of living: Affordability is on the agenda in a big way
data.description:
What do 50-year mortgages have to do with $2,000 stimulus checks? It's a way to broadcast the need for affordability when prices are still running hot. "Inflation has already exceeded the Committee's 2% target for nearly five years," Atlanta Fed President Raphael Bostic said on Wednesday. "Price stability remains the more pressing risk as signals from the labor market don't indicate a cyclical downturn clearly enough to merit significant policy loosening while inflation remains well above target."

Backdrop: Up until now, the Trump administration has primarily touted falling costs on "everything," including items from "energy" to "groceries." While that may be true compared to the percentage increases seen under Biden, or even some product categories month to month, prices are continuing to go up significantly, especially in areas that hurt the most - like food, cars, shelter, and electricity. The growing cost of living is also said to be behind recent Democratic wins, like the New York City mayoral victory by Zohran Mamdani, who built his campaign around "affordability."

While the White House has announced many measures to tackle costs in the economy, including recent plans to investigate meatpacking companies and lower prescription drugs, there is now a more focused microscope on pocketbooks and wallets. Americans do not want to see their purchasing power erode further, so a double-digit increase in meat and coffee becomes a big deal. It will also be important to show correlation, like the speech yesterday given by Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent. "Lower Treasury borrowing costs mean lower corporate borrowing costs, lower mortgage rates, and lower car payments," he said, "which all translate to greater affordability for all Americans."

A penny saved is a penny earned: While not a direct connection, the Trump administration has been broadcasting other cost-cutting agendas and fiscal discipline. Penny production officially came to an end on Wednesday, with the U.S. Mint striking its final five coins. "We're going to save the taxpayers $56M," U.S. Treasurer Brandon Beach declared after the decision to phase out the denomination.

data.title: BLOK Digital announces non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• BLOK Digital (BLOK.H:CA) on Thursday said it intends to complete a non-brokered private placement of up to 10M common shares of the company at a price of $0.10 per share for gross proceeds of up to $1M.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds to pursue the acquisition of assets or combination with a business that would enable its return to the TSX Venture Exchange, as well as for general corporate and working capital purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Michael Burry’s Scion Asset Management terminates SEC registration
data.description:
Famed investor Michael Burry's Scion Asset Management has terminated its registration status with the Securities and Exchange Commission, effective November 10, an SEC filing showed.

Several posts on X circulated a photo of a letter dated October 27 from Burry to Scion's investors saying that he was winding down his fund.

"With a heavy heart, I will liquidate the funds and return capital—but for a small audit/tax holdback – by year’s end. My estimation of value in securities is not now, and has not been for some time, in sync with the markets," the letter said.

Seeking Alpha has not independently verified the authenticity of the letter.

In this latest post on X, he also clarified details of his Palantir ((PLTR)) trade, saying his position totaled $9.2 million, not $912 million as some reports had suggested. He explained that each of the 50,000 contracts he bought at $1.84 represented 100 options to sell Palantir at $50 in 2027—a trade he executed last month.

Burry, in the same post, added that he’s now “on to much better things” set to unfold on November 25. He had previously referenced that same date in an earlier post criticizing large technology firms for allegedly inflating profits by extending the useful life of their assets, promising to share more details on November 25.

Last week, Burry’s Scion Asset Management took bearish positions in AI darlings Nvidia ((NVDA)) and Palantir ((PLTR)), as he hinted at caution toward the artificial intelligence trade that has boosted a lot of this year's market rally.

Burry, who had famously predicted the 2008 financial crisis, has been going after big tech lately. He has recently been vocal about potential risks in big tech and AI valuations. On Tuesday, he sounded a new alarm and said that major cloud and AI infrastructure companies were artificially inflating profits by extending the “useful life” of their assets.

data.title: OneWater Marine reports mixed Q4 results; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• OneWater Marine press release (ONEW): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.00 misses by $0.21.

  • Adjusted diluted earnings per share1 for fiscal fourth quarter 2025 was less than $0.01, compared to adjusted diluted loss per share1 of $(0.36) in 2024.

    Fiscal fourth quarter 2025 Adjusted EBITDA1 increased 123.0% to $17.5 million compared to $7.8 million for fiscal fourth quarter 2024.

  • Revenue of $460.14M (+21.8% Y/Y) beats by $52.56M.

  • Fiscal Year 2026 Guidance

    For fiscal full-year 2026, OneWater anticipates industry-wide unit sales to be flat year over year. When factoring in the lost revenue from exited brands, the Company expects dealership same-store sales to be flat year over year and total revenue to be in the range of $1.83 billion to $1.93 billion vs. $1.88B consensus. Adjusted EBITDA2 is expected to be in the range of $65 million to $85 million and adjusted diluted earnings per share2 is expected to be in the range of $0.25 to $0.75 vs. $1.23 consensus.

data.title: Retail hiring slows ahead of holiday season; Target rolls out new smile mandate
data.description:
Retail hiring in October fell below pre-pandemic levels for seasonal hiring, indicating weaker expectations for consumer spending this holiday season.

Retail-related job postings - including retail shops, hospitality and food services - dropped 16% in October from a year ago, Reuters reported, citing data from the Indeed Hiring Lab.

Retailers may limit hiring for the holiday season due to the added burden of tariffs, weakening consumer sentiment and the government shutdown, said Indeed Hiring Lab senior economist Daniel Culbertson.

The National Retail Federation expects retailers to hire 265,000-365,000 seasonal workers this year, compared to 442,000 seasonal hires in 2024.

"What we've seen is a lot fewer retailers go public and state that they're going to hire 'X' amount of people," said Mark Mathews, NRF chief economist, adding that companies may hire more later in the season.

Walmart (WMT), Target (TGT), Macy's (M) and UPS (UPS) have not announced their holiday hiring plans as they had done in previous years. Kroger (KR) is hiring over 18,000 seasonal workers this year, down from 25,000 last year.

But Amazon (AMZN) is hiring 250,000 people this holiday season, the same level as the last two years.

"It's possible with rate cuts and a strong showing in November, companies may make a late season push for employees, but at this point, we do not expect a strong seasonal hiring environment in 2025," said Challenger, Gray and Christmas.

Smile mandate

While Target (TGT) is yet to announce its holiday hiring plans, the retailer is hoping a new staff policy will draw more shoppers heading into the holiday season, USA Today reported.

The policy - called the 10-4 program - requires employees within 10 feet of shoppers to smile, make eye contact and wave. If shoppers come closer, within 4 feet, employees must greet them and initiate a warm helpful interaction.

"Heading into the holiday, we're making adjustments and implementing new ways to increase connection during the most important time of the year," said Adrienne Costanzo, Target's (TGT) chief stores officer.

data.title: ProSiebenSat.1 Media SE reports Q3 results
data.description:
• ProSiebenSat.1 Media SE press release (PBSFF): Q3 adjusted net income increased by EUR 60 million to EUR 91 million in the third quarter.

  • Group revenues of EUR 820 million (previous year: EUR 882 million).

data.title: Bitfarms GAAP EPS of -$0.08 misses by $0.06, revenue of $69.25M misses by $15.41M
data.description:
• Bitfarms press release (BITF): Q3 GAAP EPS of -$0.08 misses by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $69.25M (+155.8% Y/Y) misses by $15.41M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA* of $20 million, or 28% of revenue, up from $2 million or 8% of revenue in Q3 2024
  • Gross mining margin* of 35%, down from 44% in Q3 2024
  • The Company earned 520 BTC at an average Direct Cost per BTC* of $48,200
